pi_items.source: Cross-verified across setup_task.sh, export_result.sh, and verifier.py - all use identical path
Confirmed in export_result.sh (FILES array) and verifier.py (required_files list, VLM prompts). Task description states this file should show the opened Money activity.
Confirmed in export_result.sh (FILES array) and verifier.py (required_files list, VLM prompts). Task description states this file should show completed rounds.
Confirmed in export_result.sh (FILES array) and verifier.py (required_files list, VLM prompts). Task description states this file should show return to main menu.
From install_gcompris.sh and task_utils.sh: installs 'gcompris-qt' package from Ubuntu universe repository. Binary at /usr/games/gcompris-qt. Latest version is 26.0 (released Feb 4, 2026).

Task description specifies 'Money' activity. Web search confirms GCompris has a Money activity for practicing buying items and giving change using coins and bills. Activity allows clicking coins/notes at bottom and removing them from upper screen.

pi_items.verified_value: Drag coins/bills to match price; activity allows users to practice buying items, giving exact payment, and calculating change across 3 difficulty levels
pi_items.source: Web search results from GCompris project sources: 'Practice money usage' activity where users buy items and give exact price. At higher levels, multiple items with calculated total price required. Gameplay: click/tap coins or paper money at bottom to pay; click on upper screen to remove items.
Task description (task.json): 'Complete at least 3 rounds of the exercise (drag coins/bills to match the price).'

pi_items.verified_value: Ubuntu package manager (apt-get install gcompris-qt gcompris-qt-data)
pi_items.source: From install_gcompris.sh: installs via 'apt-get install -y gcompris-qt gcompris-qt-data' from Ubuntu universe repository. Binary verified at /usr/games/gcompris-qt.

privileged_info_summary: GCompris is a real open-source educational software suite (current version 26.0, released February 4, 2026) installed via Ubuntu package manager. The Money activity is a built-in GCompris activity where users practice currency handling by purchasing items and providing exact payment using coins and bills. The task requires completing 3 rounds minimum and producing 3 PNG screenshots as evidence: activity_opened.png (showing the Money activity), rounds_completed.png (showing progress/completion), and main_menu_return.png (showing the GCompris main menu with category icons). All files must be created in the specified directory (/home/ga/Documents/money_evidence) and in chronological order. The screenshots are synthetically generated by the agent during task execution; no external data files are downloaded.
